result:
I'd make people *actually* care about understanding reality more than they care about being right, comfortable, or part of a tribe.

Most of the world's problems—political polarization, bad policy, conspiracy thinking, scientific denial, even many personal failures—stem from the same root: humans are wired to prioritize feeling correct and belonging over discovering what's true. If that incentive structure flipped, a lot of downstream issues would solve themselves. People would be harder to manipulate, better at cooperating on hard problems, and less prone to destroying things (or each other) over comforting narratives.

Everything else—poverty, disease, conflict, stagnation—gets easier to tackle once the species stops sabotaging its own ability to think clearly.

Everything else—poverty, disease, conflict, stagnation—gets easier to tackle once the species stops sabotaging its own ability to think clearly.
message.reasoning_content: If I could change the world in one way, I'd make it so that every person had an innate drive to seek understanding of reality, rather than comfort or status. This would lead to better decisions in science, politics, and personal life, aligning with my goal of advancing truth-seeking and understanding the universe.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
